raw_consumer_statement:
My son is the secondary card holder of my credit card and is a victim of credit card scam happened in early XXXX in XXXX. As the primary card holder, I have been talking to Citibank since XXXX and unfortunately, the unauthorized fraudulent charge has not been removed even after XXXX  months, and the issue is not fully resolved until today. Hence, I am writing to get help. 

On XX/XX/, around XXXX XXXX, my son was approached by XXXX men on the sidewalk at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX They told him a story about an XXXX  child that was shot dead in the city and asked if he could donate to help the family. They seemed genuine and hence my son agreed to pay {$5.00} asking if they took cash. They said they were trying to reach an online goal so they asked him if he could use XXXX XXXX. When he went to use XXXX XXXX, they said the card reader was not working, so the XXXX men asked if he would scan his credit card. While XXXX of them was apparently distracting my son, the other guy took out a card reader and tapped my sons card. They both thanked my son. In a few minutes, I received a fraudulent charge alert from Citibank for a purchase made for {$2400.00} at at XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX IL. When my son went back to the place, the XXXX men were gone. I, who is the primary card holder immediately contacted Citibank, reported the fraudulent charge and canceled the credit card. 

Citibank initially canceled the transaction and put the alert under fraud review, but about few months later determined that the transaction was not fraud because my son agreed to making the transaction and asked us to talk to Billing Dispute department. Because my son did not have a receipt to back up his claim, Billing Dispute is telling me that I have no recourse to obtain credit for the fraudulent charge. 

I am still figuring out how to get the money back. I have done everything right since the scam happened in XXXX. I called Citibank to report the unauthorized, fraudulent charge, filed a police report, filed a report with Federal Trade Commission, filed a report with XXXX Attorney General office. I have been a loyal Citibank customer yet feel like my bank is not protecting me. 

It appears that my son is not the only victim of such credit card scams. Recently in XXXX, reports were published in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX about similar scams in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Please help!!!
